articles.title: Two alleged MS‑13 gang members charged in Peel human trafficking investigation
articles.summary: Peel Regional Police (PRP) say two men with alleged ties to the street gang MS‑13 (Mara Salvatrucha) have been arrested and charged in a human trafficking investigation that began last year. Investigators, working alongside the Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) and the Correctional Service of Canada (CSC), executed four search warrants in Mississauga and the Collingwood […]

articles.title: Fire at Scarborough commercial plaza being investigated as suspicious, authorities say
articles.summary: What started as a vehicle fire spread to a commercial plaza near Eglinton Avenue E. and Kennedy Road early Wednesday morning, Toronto Fire says.

articles.title: Overnight vehicle fire spreads through Scarborough commercial plaza
articles.summary: Toronto fire crews knocked down an overnight blaze that began with a vehicle fire and spread into a two‑storey commercial and industrial plaza on Eglinton Avenue East early Wednesday. Toronto Fire Services says the call came in at 2:27 a.m. for reports of a vehicle on fire at 2390 Eglinton Ave. E., near Ionview Road […]

articles.title: How wildfire smoke travels from Ontario to New York City — and turns distant city skies orange
articles.summary: Wildfires that have torched huge areas of forest and devastated communities are also sending smoke far and wide, prompting air quality warnings in distant cities and heated political rhetoric from the U.S. Experts say there's nothing particularly unusual about this summer's smoke patterns — but they say wildfires are getting worse, and that's not likely to stop on either side of the border.

articles.title: Brampton woman facing nearly 40 charges in series of rental fraud incidents
articles.summary: A Brampton woman is facing nearly 40 charges in connection to several incidents of rental fraud. Peel police say an investigation found the suspect allegedly advertised homes, including basement apartments, between May 2025 and Feb. 2026, for rent on various social media platforms. The alleged victims would sign rental agreements and send deposits for the […]

articles.title: Woman, 18, charged with attempted murder after teen stabbed on bus: Peel police
articles.summary: An 18-year-old woman has been charged with attempted murder after a teen was stabbed on board a transit bus in Mississauga, Ont, police say.

articles.title: Toronto's Pearson airport launching 2nd phase of multi-billion dollar construction project
articles.summary: Officials at Toronto’s Pearson International Airport launched the second phase of a multi-billion dollar construction project aimed at modernizing and expanding the facility on Tuesday.

articles.title: Toronto step closer to 'much needed' Liberty Village traffic and community additions
articles.summary: The City of Toronto has voted on plans to update Liberty Village's infrastructure, including a new community center, Canada Post service and school development.

articles.title: Brampton MPP accused of using public funds for ads in family newspaper, integrity commissioner reviewing
articles.summary: Ontario's integrity commissioner is reviewing allegations that a Brampton MPP used taxpayer dollars to purchase ads in a newspaper run by an immediate family member.
